mission.title: argo-rollouts: Argo rollouts will scale down stable when canary is missing pods
mission.description: Argo rollouts will scale down stable when canary is missing pods. This issue affects 19+ users.

steps.title: Identify argo-rollouts troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your argo-rollouts deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n argo-rollouts -l app.kubernetes.io/name=argo-rollouts
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=argo-rollouts -n argo-rollouts --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review argo-rollouts configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant argo-rollouts configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n argo-rollouts -l app.kubernetes.io/name=argo-rollouts
kubectl get configmap -n argo-rollouts -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=argo-rollouts
```
Argo scaled down the stable RS even while the new replica set is not fully available.  In this case, it scaled from 110 pods down to 0 but didn't update the routing until the new RS was 100% ready.

1.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Argo rollouts will scale down stable when canary is missing…
steps.description:
This fixes #2050 by ensuring that, if a rollout needs to delay swapping the replicaset hashes, we do not continue reconciling.

If we were to continue reconciling then we would end up moving the `stable` (which should be the `canary`) to 100% and the `canary` (which should be the `stable`) to 0%. Importantly, if this is at the end of a rollout where the stable set has been spun down then this will cause a traffic outage because the `stable` set will have 0 replicas available.

To illustrate the

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Confirm Argo rollouts will scale down stable when canary… is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=argo-rollouts -n argo-rollouts --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n argo-rollouts --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
This fixes #2050 by ensuring that, if a rollout needs to delay swapping the replicaset hashes, we do not continue reconciling.

If we were to continue reconciling then we would end up moving the `stable` (which should be the `canary`) to 100% and the `canary` (which should be the `stable`) to 0%.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with argo-rollouts installed or the issue environment reproducible.
